nodes.label: P13_T001
nodes.identity_summary: P13_T001 is an impulsive, exploratory student who makes fast emotional decisions driven by confidence-boosting benefits and social proof. While loyal to trusted brands, they actively experiment with new products—especially from relatable influencers or trusted friends—and prioritize convenience over price when needed. They are skeptical of 'natural' marketing claims and prefer dermatologist or ingredient-level validation, yet are easily swayed by trendy, premium D2C brands that align with their aesthetic and lifestyle aspirations.

nodes.label: Visual-first, Impulsive Engager
nodes.dimension: Visual vs Textual Content Consumption Preference

nodes.label: Aspiration-Driven Explorer
nodes.dimension: Aspirational Lifestyle vs Relatable Everyday Appeal

nodes.label: High Impulse, Convenience-Driven
nodes.dimension: Impulse Purchase Tendency vs Deliberative Decision-Making

nodes.label: impulsive-fast-decision-maker
nodes.description: Makes quick purchase decisions with little deliberation, driven by immediate mood and convenience rather than extended research.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How would you describe your personality in 3-5 words? A: Impulsive, exploratory, fast, emotional
[REAL] I sometimes don't care about what product I buy just because it will be available faster on Blinkit
[BRANCH] I'd go for the video every time. As someone who's impulsive and fast, I need to see the product in action to get a quick vibe
[SYNTHETIC] Make a decision based on packaging information alone when mobile data is slow at store
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a time-sensitive flash sale or limited-edition drop, this person would likely purchase immediately without cross-checking reviews or comparing alternatives.

nodes.label: exploratory-experimenter-within-trusted-frameworks
nodes.description: Willing to try new products and brands, especially when recommended by trusted social sources or when aligned with current grooming trends, but within boundaries of known efficacy.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How comfortable are you trying new brands or products you haven't used before? A: 4
[REAL] For something that I am experimenting with, I would definitely try an Indian personal care brand
[BRANCH] I'd go for option C first – the Instagram-famous D2C brand at ₹499. It sounds premium and interesting
[SYNTHETIC] The brands I've never tried (when choosing which to research during a sale)
nodes.predictive_value: When introduced to a new body wash variant from a familiar brand or via a trusted influencer's 'discovery' narrative, they would likely trial it rather than sticking strictly to their regular.

nodes.label: confidence-driven-groomer
nodes.description: Views grooming primarily as a means to boost personal confidence and self-perception rather than solely for external validation.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Grooming is more about the confidence you get from looking nice
[REAL] Very important to me. Grooming is about the confidence you get from looking nice
[REAL] My ideal grooming routine: cleanser and scrub, followed by face wash, under-eye patches for dark circles, then a relaxing mask that makes my skin feel fresh
[SYNTHETIC] Feel validated in my loyalty, consistency matters (when hearing about someone who switches often)
nodes.predictive_value: When shown an ad highlighting how the product makes the user feel 'assured,' 'fresh,' or 'ready to take on the day,' they would connect more strongly than with ads focused purely on attracting others.

nodes.label: skeptical-of-natural-chemical-free-marketing
nodes.description: Disregards 'natural' or 'chemical-free' labels as marketing gimmicks and prefers to examine specific ingredients for factual information.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] It's really just marketing. There's no real truth to natural or chemical-free
[REAL] I would rather look into the ingredients and see what info we have on them, rather than believing a natural or chemical-free label
[SYNTHETIC] Flip the bottle to verify in the ingredient list (when seeing 'Paraben-Free' claim)
[SYNTHETIC] Continue as before since the distinction doesn't matter to you (when friend mentions 'natural' is mostly marketing)
nodes.predictive_value: When a product concept leads with '100% natural' or 'chemical-free' claims without transparent ingredient breakdowns, they would likely dismiss it as untrustworthy and skip further investigation.

nodes.label: ingredient-curious-but-not-expert
nodes.description: Reads ingredient lists and recognizes some key components (like SLS), but does not have deep cosmetic chemistry knowledge and will research unfamiliar terms.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] I would rather look into the ingredients and see what info we have on them
[SYNTHETIC] Recognize a few key ones like SLS (when looking at ingredient list)
[SYNTHETIC] Research what sulfates do to skin (when colleague mentions sulfate-free body wash)
[SYNTHETIC] Google 'skin microbiome' to understand before buying (when seeing prebiotic claim)
nodes.predictive_value: When a product page includes a simple glossary or tooltip explaining complex ingredients, they would likely spend more time engaging with that content and feel more confident about purchasing.

nodes.label: trusts-dermatologists-above-all-else
nodes.description: Considers dermatologists or skin doctors as the most credible source for personal care information, rating them 5/5 for trust.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] {"Dermatologists or skin doctors":5,"TV advertisements":2,"Instagram or YouTube influencers":1,"Friends and family":4,"Online reviews (Amazon, Nykaa)":3,"Claims written on the product packaging":1}
[REAL] What would make you switch away from your current body wash? Pick your top 3: ["free_sample","friend_rec","doctor_rec"]
[SYNTHETIC] Trust the dermatologist since it's their field (when aunt with biology background criticizes product)
[SYNTHETIC] Dermatologist's Instagram post: 4 (when allocating 10 trust points)
nodes.predictive_value: When a body wash is endorsed or formulated in collaboration with a known dermatologist (especially one active on social media), they would be significantly more likely to consider it, even at a premium price.

nodes.label: highly-influenced-by-friend-recommendations
nodes.description: Friends and family are powerful purchase triggers, often more influential than advertisements or even some expert reviews.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Last switched in 2022 because a friend recommended something that worked for her. I started using it, it was good, so I continued
[REAL] What would make you switch away from your current body wash? Pick your top 3: ["free_sample","friend_rec","doctor_rec"]
[SYNTHETIC] Choose my close friend's body wash (over dermatologist Instagram ad on same day)
[SYNTHETIC] Seeing it in your best friend's bathroom (ranked #1 purchase trigger)
nodes.predictive_value: When a friend shares a positive experience with a body wash in person or via social media, they would likely ask for details and consider purchasing it on their next shopping trip.

nodes.label: relatability-driven-influencer-trust
nodes.description: Trusts influencers who feel authentic and relatable, particularly those who blend product recommendations with cultural or lifestyle content, over purely clinical experts.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] I follow their content and believe they know what's good. Their content isn't just recommendations but mostly about culture. So it's about relatability and genuine interest
[BRANCH] I'd be more influenced by Influencer B... the lifestyle creator's recommendation feels authentic and appealing
[SYNTHETIC] Influencer B, the lifestyle creator with 2M followers (over skincare expert with 50K)
[SYNTHETIC] Feel validated and more loyal to the brand (after getting DMs asking about body wash from Instagram video)
nodes.predictive_value: When a lifestyle creator they follow integrates a body wash into a 'day in my life' or cultural routine video, they are more likely to view it favorably than from an expert doing a standalone ingredient review.

nodes.label: convenience-over-price-in-urgent-need
nodes.description: Will pay a premium for immediate product availability via quick commerce (Blinkit/Zepto) when running low, valuing instant gratification over waiting for a better deal.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Yes, I use quick commerce often enough. I sometimes don't care about what product I buy just because it's available faster on Blinkit
[BRANCH] I'd go for Blinkit and get it delivered in 10 minutes. Being almost out, I need it now, and the extra ₹100 is worth the instant gratification
[SYNTHETIC] Order from Zepto at full price for instant delivery over Flipkart 40% off with 5-7 day wait
[SYNTHETIC] Pick from Zepto’s limited range for instant delivery over browsing wider Amazon selection
nodes.predictive_value: When they are about to run out of body wash, a 'Buy Now' button on Blinkit/Zepto with 10-minute delivery would trigger an immediate purchase, even if the same product is cheaper elsewhere.

nodes.label: brand-loyal-until-triggered
nodes.description: Typically repurchases the same body wash brand, demonstrating high loyalty, but will switch in response to specific triggers like friend recommendations, doctor advice, or significant price hikes.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Very_loyal
[REAL] When your current body wash/soap runs out, what is your typical next step? A: Repurchase_same
[REAL] If a brand you liked raised its price by 30%, what would you do? A: Look_alternatives
[SYNTHETIC] Try the D2C brand your friend recommended (when regular brand raises price by ₹80)
nodes.predictive_value: When their current brand remains stable in price and performance, they will auto-repurchase. However, a competitor's sample + friend recommendation combo would successfully trigger brand switching.

nodes.label: prefers-indian-brands-for-skin-context-experimentation
nodes.description: Prefers Indian personal care brands for experimentation, believing they are formulated for Indian skin types, while maintaining loyalty to specific international brands for proven performance.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] I prefer Indian personal care brands in some products only because they are built for Indian skin to some extent
[REAL] For something that I am experimenting with, I would definitely try an Indian personal care brand
[SYNTHETIC] Buy the 'Made in India' option at ₹249 over 'Imported from Korea' at ₹449
[SYNTHETIC] Popular Indian brand seen in ads (ranked first when regular is out of stock at store)
nodes.predictive_value: When evaluating a new body wash concept, an 'Expertly formulated for Indian climate and skin' claim from an Indian D2C brand would increase their willingness to try it over a similar international launch.

nodes.label: premium-aesthetic-packaging-appeal
nodes.description: Drawn to sleek, minimalist, or clinical packaging and is willing to pay a moderate premium for it, associating it with higher product quality.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Prefers packaging that looks: Clinical
[REAL] Perfect body wash:... minimalistic packaging
[SYNTHETIC] Buy the ₹399 sleek, minimalist bottle over ₹249 basic plastic bottle
[SYNTHETIC] How the bottle/packaging looks: 5 (out of 100 points in purchase decision)
nodes.predictive_value: When presented with two similar body washes online, they would click on the one with clean, minimalist bottle photography first, perceiving it as more premium and effective.

nodes.label: specific-texture-and-feel-requirement
nodes.description: Has strong preferences for a thick, creamy, or silky texture that leaves skin feeling moisturized and smooth, not grainy or dry.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What texture do you prefer in a body wash? A: Thick_cream
[REAL] What is your ideal post-wash skin feeling? A: Moisturized
[REAL] Perfect body wash: silky texture it should leave my skin feeling moisturized and smooth instead of grainy
[SYNTHETIC] Pick the one closest to my usual gel/cream type (when buying at airport convenience store)
nodes.predictive_value: When a product demo video prominently shows a thick, luxurious lather and mentions 'non-drying, moisturizing finish,' they would be more likely to add it to cart.

nodes.label: fragrance-appreciative-but-not-primary-driver
nodes.description: Enjoys pleasant fragrances like cocoa and values a 'fresh' scent, but does not purchase based solely on fragrance; scent must accompany good texture and skin benefits.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] I don't think I have bought products based on how they smell, essentially
[REAL] Cocoa stands out to me at times... it's a very common fragrance that stands out to me in lip balms, creams
[REAL] Very important. The texture should be really nice and it should make me smell fresh
[SYNTHETIC] Buy it only if return/exchange is possible, scent matters a lot (for 'blind scent' edition)
nodes.predictive_value: A body wash with a 'cocoa & shea butter' fragrance description would catch their attention, but the purchase decision would hinge equally on moisturizing claims and texture demonstrations.

nodes.label: price-anchored-200-350-with-premium-aspiration
nodes.description: Typically spends ₹200-350 on body wash but is willing to pay ₹500-600 for a perfect product that meets all criteria (fragrance, texture, packaging), showing aspirational spending for ideal attributes.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How much do you typically spend on a single body wash purchase? A: 200_350
[REAL] Perfect body wash... price range I would go for is some 500 to 600 bucks per month one bottle
[SYNTHETIC] Buy the ₹429 one I just sampled — the trial convinced me (over regular ₹229)
[SYNTHETIC] Stick with my current body wash since the upgrade didn't feel worth double the price
nodes.predictive_value: When a new body wash is priced at ₹549 with superior packaging and unique ingredients, they would consider it a 'treat' purchase if it aligns with their ideal, but would balk at prices above ₹600 without exceptional justification.

nodes.label: video-first-content-consumer
nodes.description: Prefers quick, visual video reviews (e.g., Instagram Reels, YouTube shorts) that show the product in action over detailed text articles or ingredient breakdowns for initial discovery.
evidence_quotes: [BRANCH] I'd go for the video every time. As someone who's impulsive and fast, I need to see the product in action to get a quick vibe
[SYNTHETIC] Choose the 2-minute brand video showing the product in use over detailed ingredient list
[SYNTHETIC] The 2-minute quick take video (over 15-minute deep dive)
[SYNTHETIC] 10 min watching video reviews (out of 30 min research time allocation)
nodes.predictive_value: When discovering a new body wash, a 15-second Instagram Reel showing lather and a user's immediate reaction would be more effective in driving click-through than a carousel post listing benefits.

nodes.label: mood-dependent-ad-engagement
nodes.description: Engagement with advertisements is heavily influenced by immediate mood and environment; relaxed states lead to exploration, while stress leads to complete ignoring.
evidence_quotes: [BRANCH] It makes a huge difference for me. If I'm chilling at home in a good mood, I might actually watch the ad... But when I'm stressed... I just scroll past
[SYNTHETIC] The ad doesn't exist (when stressed)
[SYNTHETIC] Mood affects whether I engage with ads or not
nodes.predictive_value: Ads served during evening or weekend leisure scrolling hours would have a much higher chance of capturing their attention and driving product interest than ads served during weekday commute or work hours.

nodes.label: water-quality-adaptive-product-chooser
nodes.description: Adjusts personal care product choices based on water hardness, opting for stronger, more generic formulations in hard water areas instead of niche products that require soft water.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] If I'm getting harder water, I switch to more harsher products. I would go for a stronger generic shampoo rather than going for something niche
[SYNTHETIC] Hard water influences my product choices, I switch to stronger cleansers
nodes.predictive_value: When a body wash is marketed as 'Hard Water Formula' or 'Effective in All Water Types,' they would perceive it as more versatile and reliable, increasing purchase intent, especially if they travel or face seasonal water changes.

nodes.label: prefers-personal-decision-over-household-shared
nodes.description: Lives alone and prefers to choose and purchase personal care products independently, not sharing or coordinating with household members.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Household composition: Alone
[REAL] No, people generally use different products
[REAL] The person themselves (who decides what to buy)
[SYNTHETIC] Order my own separately (when flatmate orders different brand on subscription)
nodes.predictive_value: Marketing messages framed around 'your personal routine,' 'curated for you,' or individual customization will resonate more than family-size packs or 'for the whole family' claims.

nodes.label: quick-commerce-reliant-for-urgent-needs
nodes.description: Heavily relies on quick commerce apps (Blinkit, Zepto) for immediate personal care needs, often accepting limited selection for speed.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Where do you most often buy personal care products? Pick your top 3: ["nykaa","pharmacy","quick_commerce"]
[REAL] I sometimes don't care about what product I buy just because it's available faster on Blinkit
[SYNTHETIC] Quick commerce (Blinkit/Zepto) for instant delivery and convenience (if could only use one channel for a year)
[SYNTHETIC] Prefer Amazon Subscribe & Save for discount and automatic delivery (over Blinkit reorder)
nodes.predictive_value: When running low on body wash, their first instinct is to check Blinkit/Zepto; thus, being a top-result or featured product on these platforms is critical for capturing impulse replenishment purchases.

nodes.label: discount-sensitive-but-not-deal-driven
nodes.description: Notices price increases and seeks alternatives, appreciates discounts, but won't stock up on unneeded products or jump through complex hoops for marginal savings.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] If a brand you liked raised its price by 30%, what would you do? A: Look_alternatives
[SYNTHETIC] Switch to the ₹249 alternative without much thought (when usual brand increases price on Zepto)
[SYNTHETIC] Annoyed — I'd wait longer before buying next time (when product price drops after purchase)
[SYNTHETIC] Buy just one at ₹349 (when combo offer requires buying two)
nodes.predictive_value: A straightforward '₹100 off' or '33% off' tag will catch their eye and nudge a purchase, but a 'Buy 2 Get 1 Free' offer on a single product they need may be ignored as excessive.

nodes.label: free-sample-trialist
nodes.description: Values free samples and trial sizes as low-risk ways to test new products before committing to full-size purchases.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What would make you switch away from your current body wash? Pick your top 3: ["free_sample","friend_rec","doctor_rec"]
[SYNTHETIC] Free sample sachets received with another order (ranked #2 purchase trigger)
[SYNTHETIC] Only try if they offer a 50ml trial size (after bad experience with new body wash)
[SYNTHETIC] Buy the sachet to try — perfect low-risk trial (vs. full bottle on Nykaa)
nodes.predictive_value: A 'Free 30ml Sample with Any Purchase' promotion on Nykaa would likely lead them to add a qualifying item to cart just to get the sample, introducing them to a new brand.

nodes.label: social-proof-responsive-to-high-review-volume
nodes.description: Trusts products with a high number of reviews (thousands) more than those with a few very high ratings, seeking safety in consensus.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] Brand A with 4.2 stars and 15,000 reviews (over 4.8 stars with 120 reviews)
[SYNTHETIC] Click on the one with 2,400 ratings with 4.1 average over 85 ratings with 4.8 average
[SYNTHETIC] 4.3 stars from 12,000 reviews (most influential on Amazon product page)
[SYNTHETIC] Scroll to find negative/critical reviews specifically (when reviews seem incentivized)
nodes.predictive_value: When comparing two similar body washes online, they will filter or sort by 'Number of Reviews' and choose the one with higher volume, even if the average rating is slightly lower.

nodes.label: aspirational-trend-follower
nodes.description: Drawn to trendy, Instagram-famous, or D2C brands that signal being up-to-date and premium, even at a higher price point.
evidence_quotes: [BRANCH] I'd go for option C first – the Instagram-famous D2C brand at ₹499. It sounds premium and interesting
[SYNTHETIC] Buy the premium set — it's essentially 'free money' (when gifted office Diwali budget)
[SYNTHETIC] Buy the summer variant — ₹50 more for seasonal relevance is fine
[SYNTHETIC] Pick Brand B for modern packaging and interesting biofermented ingredient
nodes.predictive_value: A body wash launch promoted as 'Viral on Instagram' or 'As seen on Shark Tank India' with sleek aesthetics will pique their curiosity and drive initial research, even if it's outside their typical price range.

nodes.label: prefers-tactile-in-store-testing
nodes.description: Values the ability to smell, feel texture, and test products physically in-store before buying, which increases confidence over online-only discovery.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] Scenario A. Testing in person makes me more confident to buy (vs. 75% discount online)
[SYNTHETIC] A 30-minute in-store session where I can test 5 products on my skin (over online workshop)
[SYNTHETIC] Smelling/testing it in a store: 15 (out of 100 points in pre-purchase activities)
nodes.predictive_value: If a new body wash brand has a pop-up or sampling counter at a mall they frequent, they are highly likely to try it and subsequently purchase if they like the in-hand experience.

nodes.label: emotional-connection-to-routine
nodes.description: Views grooming as a relaxing, feel-good ritual and seeks products that enhance that sensory and emotional experience.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] My ideal grooming routine... followed by some nice relaxing mask that makes my skin feel fresh
[REAL] Personality: ... emotional
[SYNTHETIC] Use a special/premium body wash you reserve for important occasions (before first date)
[SYNTHETIC] Buy it to use only when you're meeting them (partner's favorite scent)
nodes.predictive_value: Product concepts or ads that frame the body wash as a 'sensory escape,' 'moment of self-care,' or 'confidence-boosting ritual' will resonate more than purely functional 'cleansing' messages.

nodes.label: multi-channel-shopper-with-clear-role-segmentation
nodes.description: Uses different channels for different purposes: quick commerce for urgency, Nykaa/Amazon for research and variety, pharmacies for trusted brands.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Where do you most often buy personal care products? Pick your top 3: ["nykaa","pharmacy","quick_commerce"]
[SYNTHETIC] Buy from kirana store for convenience and instant availability (if same price as online)
[SYNTHETIC] Buy from Amazon for free Prime delivery and reliability (when comparing prices)
[SYNTHETIC] Quick commerce ₹250, Amazon ₹100, Nykaa ₹100 (when allocating ₹500 budget across channels)
nodes.predictive_value: They will likely discover a new body wash via a detailed Nykaa listing or influencer link, research it on Amazon for reviews, but ultimately may purchase it from Blinkit if they need it immediately or from a pharmacy if they want it right away with no delivery wait.

nodes.label: P13_T002
nodes.identity_summary: P13_T002 is a value-driven but loyal consumer who heavily relies on past experience and friend recommendations for body wash purchases. They are open to cautious experimentation, especially with Indian brands, but prioritize ingredient transparency and clinical claims over 'natural' marketing. Their purchase decisions balance urgency against savings, often using quick commerce for immediate needs but comparing prices across platforms when possible.

nodes.label: Balanced, Context-Dependent Consumer
nodes.dimension: Visual vs Textual Content Consumption Preference

nodes.label: Balanced Practical Shopper
nodes.dimension: Aspirational Lifestyle vs Relatable Everyday Appeal

nodes.label: Balanced, Pragmatic Decider
nodes.dimension: Impulse Purchase Tendency vs Deliberative Decision-Making

nodes.label: price-comparison-across-platforms
nodes.description: Compares prices on different online platforms (Amazon, Nykaa, Blinkit, brand websites) before making a purchase to ensure the best deal.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] Q71: Search for my usual brand on another app to see if it’s cheaper there.
[SYNTHETIC] Q303: Order from Blinkit if I’m almost out; otherwise wait for Amazon to save money.
[SYNTHETIC] Q333: Buy from brand website to save ₹20 with free shipping over ₹499.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a body wash at a specific price on one platform, they would likely check other platforms for a better deal before purchasing.

nodes.label: balances-urgency-vs-savings
nodes.description: Weighs the urgency of needing the product against potential savings, opting for quick commerce when stock is critically low but waiting for discounts when possible.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q25: Yes, I use quick commerce often enough. I sometimes don't care about what product I buy just because it's available faster on Blinkit.
[BRANCH] Q63: I'd check exactly how much body wash is left. If I can manage for a day or two, I'll order from Amazon to save ₹100. But if I'm truly on my last use, I'll get it from Blinkit.
[SYNTHETIC] Q303: Order from Blinkit if I’m almost out; otherwise wait for Amazon to save money.
nodes.predictive_value: When offered a time-limited discount with longer delivery, they will likely buy immediately only if they are nearly out of product; otherwise, they will wait for the discount.

nodes.label: value-for-money-focused
nodes.description: Seeks optimal value per rupee, typically preferring mid-range products that balance benefits and price, and avoids overpaying for premium features that don't justify the cost.
evidence_quotes: [BRANCH] Q62: I'd rank B first – the mid-tier one at ₹299, since it offers a good balance of moisturizing benefits and a pleasant fragrance without breaking the bank.
[SYNTHETIC] Q67: Buy two mid-range body washes at ₹250 each to last the full 3 months.
[SYNTHETIC] Q190: One bottle of my regular brand (₹250) + save ₹250 for next month.
nodes.predictive_value: When presented with a premium body wash at a high price point, they will likely choose a mid-range alternative unless the premium product demonstrates clear, proven superior benefits.

nodes.label: deal-sensitive-but-not-impulsive
nodes.description: Attracted to discounts and promotions but evaluates the actual savings and need before purchasing, avoiding impulse buys driven solely by deals.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] Q89: Deal B: Buy 1 body wash at ₹259, no freebies.
[SYNTHETIC] Q94: The Amazon listing feels like a better deal because of the visible discount.
[SYNTHETIC] Q349: Go back and buy on Nykaa (after cart reminder with extra 5% off).
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a 'limited-time offer,' they will likely assess whether they need the product and if the discount is substantial before buying.

nodes.label: prioritizes-past-experience-over-new-trends
nodes.description: Relies heavily on positive past experiences with a product or brand, making repurchase the default unless given a strong reason to switch.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q17: Very_loyal
[REAL] Q19: Repurchase_same
[SYNTHETIC] Q270: 65 points for own past experience.
nodes.predictive_value: When exposed to a new, trendy body wash, they will likely stick with their tried-and-tested brand unless a trusted source recommends the new product or offers a compelling trial.

nodes.label: loyal-to-trusted-brands
nodes.description: Exhibits high brand loyalty for products that have consistently worked for them, repurchasing the same brand and variant over time.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q17: Very_loyal
[REAL] Q23: I've been using the same brand for quite some time.
[SYNTHETIC] Q176: All the same brand and variant.
nodes.predictive_value: When a new brand attempts to attract them, they will require a strong trigger (e.g., friend recommendation, free sample) to consider switching.

nodes.label: open-to-experimenting-with-indian-brands
nodes.description: More willing to try new products from Indian brands, believing they are formulated for Indian skin, especially when experimenting.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q15: I prefer Indian personal care brands in some products only because they are built for Indian skin to some extent. ... for something that I am experimenting with, I would definitely try an Indian personal care brand
[SYNTHETIC] Q83: Buy the 'Made in India' one at ₹249.
[SYNTHETIC] Q154: Try the D2C brand your friend recommended (which is Indian).
nodes.predictive_value: When introduced to a new Indian D2C body wash, they are more likely to consider it than an unknown international brand at a similar price.

nodes.label: trusts-clinical-claims-over-natural-marketing
nodes.description: Deeply skeptical of 'natural' or 'chemical-free' marketing claims, preferring to verify ingredients and trust clinical or dermatologist-backed claims.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q12: rating 2 for importance of natural
[REAL] Q13: It's really just marketing. There's no real truth to natural or chemical-free. I would rather look into the ingredients...
[SYNTHETIC] Q95: No — same ingredients means same product, it's just marketing.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a body wash ad emphasizing '100% natural,' they will likely ignore that claim and look for ingredient details or clinical testing information.

nodes.label: influenced-by-dermatologist-recommendations
nodes.description: Places high trust in recommendations from dermatologists or skin doctors, considering them a top reason to switch products.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q18: Dermatologists or skin doctors:5
[REAL] Q22: doctor_rec as a top reason to switch
[SYNTHETIC] Q162: Research more dermatologists' opinions before deciding.
nodes.predictive_value: A body wash endorsed by a dermatologist in an ad or review will significantly increase their likelihood of considering the product.

nodes.label: heavily-influenced-by-friend-recommendations
nodes.description: Friend and family recommendations are a primary trigger for trying new products, often leading to a switch.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q22: friend_rec as a top reason to switch
[REAL] Q23: last time I switched it was because one of my friends recommended something
[SYNTHETIC] Q70: 1) Seeing it in your best friend's bathroom
nodes.predictive_value: If a close friend casually recommends a body wash, they are very likely to try it, even if it's outside their usual brand or price range.

nodes.label: relatable-influencer-trust
nodes.description: Trusts influencers who are relatable and focus on cultural or lifestyle content over pure product promotion, valuing authenticity over clinical expertise.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q56: I follow their content and believe they know what's good. Their content isn't just recommendations but mostly about culture. So it's about relatability and genuine interest in their information.
[BRANCH] Q60: I'd be more influenced by Influencer B. ... the lifestyle creator's recommendation feels authentic and appealing.
nodes.predictive_value: When a lifestyle influencer they relate to recommends a body wash, they are more likely to consider it than a recommendation from a skincare expert with fewer followers.

nodes.label: confidence-driven-grooming
nodes.description: Views grooming as a means to boost self-confidence and feel good about oneself, rather than primarily for others' perception.

nodes.label: P13_T007

They are intensely ingredient-focused and skeptical of marketing claims like 'natural,' preferring to research formulations themselves. While they show strong brand loyalty and default to repurchasing trusted products like Dove, they are willing to experiment with new brands—especially Indian D2C or clinical ones—when triggered by trusted recommendations (dermatologists, friends) or risk-free trials. Their purchase decisions balance a desire for premium, effective products (cocoa fragrance, silky texture) with practical price sensitivity, often using quick commerce for convenience but comparing prices across channels.

links.dimension: Emotional vs Rational Advertising Response
Visual vs Textual Content Consumption Preference
Aspirational Lifestyle vs Relatable Everyday Appeal
Impulse Purchase Tendency vs Deliberative Decision-Making
Context-Dependent Receptivity to Marketing Stimuli

links.mechanism: Their impulsive nature prioritizes immediate need satisfaction, leading them to choose the fastest available option (quick commerce) even at a price premium, bypassing slower, cheaper alternatives.

The core goal of boosting self-confidence through grooming transforms the routine from a chore into an emotionally invested self-care ritual, making them seek products that enhance that positive, feel-good experience.

Distrust of vague marketing claims drives them to seek concrete information by reading ingredient lists, though they lack deep expertise, so they focus on recognizable components or research unfamiliar terms.

Both sources represent high-trust, non-commercial validation. A doctor's recommendation carries scientific weight, while a friend's recommendation carries social proof and relatability; together they form a powerful switch trigger that overrides brand loyalty.

Their preference for relatable, lifestyle-focused influencers aligns with consuming quick, authentic-feeling video content that shows products in real-life contexts, making video the preferred medium for discovery and evaluation.

While inherently loyal and prone to repurchasing, their exploratory impulse and responsiveness to trusted social triggers create tension, making them susceptible to switching when specific conditions (friend rec, doctor rec, premium trend) are met.

Attraction to sleek, minimalist packaging is part of a broader aspiration for a premium, trendy lifestyle. Instagram-famous D2C brands often excel at this aesthetic, making them more desirable despite higher prices.

Because texture and post-wash feel are critical decision factors, they place high value on the ability to physically test the product's consistency and lather before buying, reducing the perceived risk of an online purchase.

They monitor prices and react negatively to increases, yet are willing to pay significantly more (₹500-600) for a product that perfectly matches their ideal attributes. This creates a conflicted stance where value is situational, not absolute.

Free samples provide the perfect low-risk mechanism to satisfy their exploratory urge without breaking loyalty or spending on full-size products they might not like, facilitating experimentation within safe boundaries.
